reviews.review_msg: Great professor. He is a fair marker but he requires that you put effort into his classes to get that A+.
worst and mean-est prof i ever had. can never have discussion or negotiation with him.  take his courses on your own risk!

this is one of the hardest econ courses...but i think he did a good job going over material slowly and clearly.  however, mt(55 avg i think...?) and final were hard...but passable if u put in the time studyin.  i dont' like the fact he doesn't curve on a grade tho...especially for a 5credit core econ course

Hard course no doubt, but his grading system makes it even harder! ***NOT CURVED*** Ex.) If average = 50%, if curved you get C+; according to his grading 50% = F. His system works IF the course is easier, but 305 is a hard course, avg on midterms will be much lower ! If Prof you are reading this, pls check your system for teaching 305!
His writing sucks ... always waste time for guessing what he is writing ... and always can't understand wht he is asking in assignment and midterm ... but he is HOT ...HANDSOME ar !
He is the BEST Econ Prof! The only prof who is willing to spend that extra effort to help you out before the exam.
he sucks at teachin..can't read his notes..
Nice guy, very willing to help.

Took 2nd time w/him after 382. Excellent prof. NO QUESTION. Only problem of this course is having 301 as pre-requisite and not some Stat course instead! Too much STAT concepts used and NOT covering enough STAT concepts in class! I could see students having hard time w/ this course due to NO STAT BACKGROUND! Other than that fair marking on Final.
best prof ever!! a super easy course
His lecture notes is well-organized. Just that I prefer powerpoint notes as his hand-writing is reli hard to read. Overall, one of the best Profs @ econ department.
He is great. He never comes to class late. He puts efforts in teaching as well as encouraging students to answer questions. Well-prepared. I just wonder why he is hired in limited terms.

Workplace Participation - not sure how this is an Econ course but it was all concepts (words), no math. Just need you to read all materials and MEMORIZE them WELL. Doesn't grade on curve. Easy course overall, memorize all material, A- or B+ should not be a problem.
No Comments
He is the best prof. ever!!
This course is the easiest course of all 300 level. Pay attention on lecture notes.
Unclear Lectures, easy to pass, hard to get A
The best proff...easy to get a A plus...

his in-class notes are great, plus read the textbook that correspond.. he focuses more on qualitative stuff.. not quantitative mathematical questions..  too bad for ESL students.. but yea he's lazy ;)
No Comments

pretty decent prof! His midterm was brutal... it was something like 9/120 students got an A and 50 something failed but then his final made it up.  I like his accent and his fashion sense .. . Overall, just study his notes cuz some of his questions are DIRECTLY from it.
wow~amazingly well prof! he is pretty good looking, and his lecture notes are very informative...however, he is a lazy prof =P (*hint: find past exam and study them, u'll ace all of the exams)

Lectures were boring, lots of notes taking but you'll have a hard time understanding his handwriting. His exam format is: 60% REALLY EASY question, then just bomb the whole class with one question. It would be better if it goes on a curve though, since everyone bombs that question.

Final is harder than I expected, but it's not mission impossible. The foundation is supply-demand, plus some new concepts related to labor markets. This course is really boring itself. In my opinion, he taught well.

Summer 2005. His Final is really hard. It is impossible to pass the final exam. Hopefully he will scale down the final. Overall the class should be a easy one. His midterm and assignments are quite easy.

VERY NICE, PATIENT PROF!! His teaching style is awesome, easy tests and NO CURVES!!! Although the no curving part makes it hard to get an A+ or an A, but if you are aiming for a B+ or more, just take courses with him!

He is a patient and helpful person.. i like wut he wears every lec. His teaching style is also awesome. He only cares about how much you understand and how much time you spend on this course, and then you will get a good effort. He marking is really fair. Trust me!!
Very good prof!!  He's always happy to talk and help solving problems.  Easy going & quick response by emails!  I did learn something from the course.

Thorough notes, clear applications of theories learned in class. Best of all, on the exam questions, he makes all assumptions and conditions explicitly stated, so you know exactly what he's asking, unlike many other sfu econ profs. Only qualm is he needs to get better control over the class.

I have no pressure at all from taking this prof. Although getting a good grade is not easy from him, however, what you need is just studying from his notes and you will be fine for the exams. I am taking 381 with him next sem, anyone joining?
Nice prof but need to improve on his teaching style. Thorough notes but only if you can read his writing..mt and final was more application of the stuff learnt..ie. scenario based

What a disaster: I am failing an special topic course. I got an A in ECON 381, a similar course, but this prof writes tricky exams while only teaching us basics in lectures. Moreover, the textbook does not allow us to think at advanced levels to prepare us. He has a wonderful wardrobe.
Be prepared to copy a lot of notes during his class. His handwriting his horrendous and he doesn't teach very well. As well, his midterms are quite tricky. Though he does dress quite decent.
clear, right to the point...easy...just study his notes and that's the exam

if u r planning to take his class...make sure u take him as ur TA as well...actually his teaching isnt bad..and he knows his stuff well..but the only thing he lacks is his communicative skills..he teaches as if he is talking to himself and that's why he can teach much better in smaller classes..
only need to stay home study the powerpoint notes and read the text.  no need attend lecture since can't even understand his english.
spends too much time with female students. boring and incompetent
Useless to attend lectures. Dunno how to teach.
unclear lecture,just stay home and read the text, attending his lecture is just wasting your time since you won't understand his english
lectures are very unclear, u can't hear him cuz he mumbles. not really worth going to lectures either, he reads the powerpoint from the textbook's website!!

For some unknown reasons, i got an A+ even though i didnt do good on the quizzes/midterm. But i agree that he is a better TA then a professor.  So the boring lectures are optional as long as u r willing to read the txt.
bad lecture, just read out the power point which everyone can get it thru the web site of the text
good prof., easy to talk to and willing to help.
Dr. Hobdari is a very good instructor. He is always very well prepared and also very helpful when there is a problem.
No Comments
Good guy, easy to talk to and willing to explain things.  Uses PowerPoint slides from textbook website, so lecture seemed fairly optional.  Frequent quizzes and homework kept me on my toes.
his lectures r unclear coz he mumbles all the time...but actually his teaching isnt bad if u get the chance to know him from being in his tutorial sections...he's a better TA than he is a prof...
So So!
he just reads off the powerpoint from the textbook's website. speaks unclearly so no one attends lectures anyway
Lectures follow text really closely.  If you read the text this course will be easy, but if you don't it'll be really tough.  He has strong background knowledge and is really helpful and friendly.
No Comments

he teaches from the powerpoint that's available on the textbook's website. so pretty much, he reads the notes and TRIES to explain but he's horrible at teaching. u'll be bored out of your mind, plus his accent makes listening even more difficult.
He just uses the textbook's powerpoint, and doesn't know how to teach. Exams and assignments are tricky. He should learn how to teach before he teaches everyone.
What he said is on the text's powerpoint. Read the text is preferable. Homeworks and quizzes are tricky and hard. Good at economics knowledge but bad at teaching.
Well. make sure u pay attention to what he says or else u r going to fall asleep. Very tricky quizzes/hmwk and quite tricky midterms.
